About: It is about cartoon Venezuelan people driving.
It is about Venezuelan people driving with important Venezuelans from history behind the wheel.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the Central University of Venezuela.
It is associated with the University City of Caracas campus.
It is associated with Pedro León Zapata.
It is associated with Carlos Raúl Villanueva.
It is associated with Diego Rivera.
BelongsTo: It belongs to all of Caracas.
BuiltIn: It was built in the late 1990s.
ConsistsOf: It consists of stoneware tiles each 20 x 20 cm.
CreatedBy: It was created by cartoonist Pedro León Zapata.
CreatedFor: It was created for drivers stuck in traffic.
It was created for the university.
It was created for the municipal mayor Antonio Ledezma.
CreatedTo: It was created to bring some color to the university.
HasA: It has experienced deterioration since its construction.
It has been the focus of restorers.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by being large and popular.
It is characterized by its cartoon style.
It is characterized by its critical tone towards the government.
It is characterized by depicting the everyday reality of life for regular Venezuelans.
It is characterized by its association with the Synthesis of the Arts ideology.
It is characterized by its message concerned with Venezuela's social present.
It is characterized by being an open-air gallery.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by the prevailing modernism in Latin America.
It was influenced by the Synthesis of the Arts ideology.
It was influenced by Diego Rivera's mural method.
IsA: It is a giant ceramic mural.
It is a caricature done by a painter.
It is one of the largest murals in Latin America.
KnownAs: It is known as Conductores del país.
KnownFor: It is known for depicting historic figures like Simón Bolívar, Simón Rodríguez, Teresa de la Parra, Armando Reverón, and José María Vargas driving vehicles.
LocatedIn: It is located on a wall outside the Covered Gymnasium at the Central University of Venezuela.
It is located facing out to the Francisco Fajardo freeway.
It is located at the northeast boundary of the University City of Caracas campus.
ManufacturedBy: It was manufactured by the company Cerámica Carabobo.
PartOf: It is part of the Central University of Venezuela's University City of Caracas campus.
It is part of an "open-air gallery".
ProducedBy: It was produced by ceramist Ricardo Ceruzzi.
It was produced using single-firing furnaces.
UsedAs: It is used as a boundary marker for the University City of Caracas campus.
UsedFor: It is used for its artistic purpose.
It is used for its functional purpose.
It is used for entertainment of drivers stuck in traffic.
UsedIn: It is used in the Central University of Venezuela.
UsedTo: It is used to give residents of Caracas a "sense of belonging".
